verdict: MIXED-PASS-WITH-CAVEAT. Source-domain (varves) is solidly covered by 3 authoritative references. LLM-side coverage is shallow: the search returned checkpointing-systems and unrelated clinical work, none of which proposes parameter-delta archeology. The primary agent's suspicion is well-founded: this query set omits the directly adjacent literature on influence functions (Koh & Liang; TRAK; datamodels), membership inference (Shokri et al.; Carlini et al.), and training-data extraction (Carlini extracting training data from LLMs; Nasr et al. 'Scalable Extraction'). Those fields explicitly attempt backward inference of training data from model state and would significantly weaken the novelty claim. The mechanical PASS on varve refs masks an under-searched LLM side; novelty assessment cannot be concluded without a second-pass query targeting influence-function / MIA / extraction literature.
